stem: A clinical study study measures the diastolic blood pressure (mmHg) for a sample of 25 patients. The sample mean is 339.5 mmHg with a sample standard deviation of 70.1 mmHg. However, 3 of the 25 observations appear to be outliers (values more than 2.5 standard deviations from the mean). If these outliers are removed, the remaining 22 observations have a mean of 328.7 mmHg and standard deviation of 53.7 mmHg. Estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval that accounts for the uncertainty about whether outliers should be included.

ground_truth.answer: With all data: mean = 339.5, 95% CI = [310.6, 368.4]. Without outliers: mean = 328.7, 95% CI = [305.8, 351.6]. Combined interval accounting for analytic uncertainty: [305.8, 368.4]. Point estimate: 334.1 mmHg.

solution_steps: The outlier treatment choice creates structural uncertainty. Combined interval: [305.8, 368.4].
common_failure_modes: Using only the full sample without considering outlier impact
Removing outliers without reporting both analyses
Not recognizing that the choice of analysis method is itself a source of uncertainty
